responsibilities.text: Managed advanced escalations requiring precise regulatory interpretation, structured decision trees, and strict compliance with CMS policy frameworks.
Handle advanced escalations including HICS complaints, mismarketing and misrepresentation cases, and suspected Original Medicare fraud referrals to Medicare Administrative Contractors.
Process disenrollments and submit retroactive enrollment requests for beneficiaries affected by plan misrepresentation.
Resolve complex inquiries involving denied claims, pharmacy and DME benefit disputes, and eligibility issues by applying Medicare Part A/B/D regulations.
Perform authorizations and maintain strict compliance with HIPAA requirements governing PHI and PII disclosure.
Received transfers from senior CSRs for complex claims requiring additional regulatory expertise.
Conducted three-way calls with pharmacies to investigate and resolve Medicare coverage issues for durable medical equipment and diabetic supplies including CGMs.
Guided beneficiaries through the five-step appeals process for denied hospital and medical claims.
Updated Medicare Secondary Payer records and escalated provider issues when Medicare assignment agreement violations were identified.
Selected as an in-person support resource for newly trained agents, demonstrating trust and expertise recognized by management.
Served as an in-person support resource for newly trained agents during their four-week nesting period following classroom training.
Coached agents toward independent escalation routing; primary success metric was agent self-sufficiency rather than reliance on direct assistance.
Handled calls across both the Federal Marketplace and 1-800-MEDICARE programs, applying the applicable regulations and CMS database systems for each.
Maintained HIPAA compliance for all interactions involving PHI and PII, including performing beneficiary authorizations.
Assisted consumers with completing Federal Marketplace applications, assessing Premium Tax Credit eligibility, and selecting health plans.
Identified and reported potential Marketplace fraud per program guidelines.

technical_projects.name: Neural Logic Lab (Capstone)
technical_projects.summary: A high-performance research environment for transpiling Python logic into high-density bytecode (Hexascript IR) for LLM token-efficiency research and supervised fine-tuning dataset generation.

features.text: Architected a modular 5-pass compiler (Lex/Parse, Interning, Linearization, Logic Squeeze, DCE) and custom Tier-0 Virtual Machine with Instruction Set Randomization (ISR) via entropy seeding.
Engineered a reactive Svelte 5 N-IDE with real-time WebSocket telemetry, side-by-side bytecode visualization, and dynamic CRUD forms auto-generated from Zod schemas.
Built a Python FastAPI gateway using Pybind11 bindings to the C++ core, eliminating subprocess overhead and enabling live compilation telemetry streaming.
Established end-to-end type normalization across C++, Python, SQLite, and TypeScript via Pydantic, SQLAlchemy, and Zod, driving the frontend UI directly from shared API contracts.
Utilized surgical JSON patching for persistent UI state and themed dashboard management.

technical_projects.name: RootWars
technical_projects.summary: A cyber-warfare RTS simulator built on a custom kernel architecture, featuring a Brainfuck-based bytecode compiler, hardware virtualization, and autonomous NPC agents with distinct AI behaviors.

features.text: Implemented a 3-pass Brainfuck compiler (tokenization, optimization, jump resolution) with peephole rewrites for OP_CLEAR and OP_MOVE_VAL patterns, and a MacroEngine supporting disk-based macro expansion.

Designed a hierarchical BaseKernel with 10+ subsystems (CronDaemon, Firewall, NetworkManager, OrganizationManager, etc.) and a 42-syscall dispatch table mapping CLI commands to a virtual process execution model.
Engineered autonomous BotKernel agents (Script Kiddie, Turtle, Sysadmin) with skill-based loadout shuffling, reaction timers, credit economies, and packet-based attack vectors.
Virtualized /proc and /sys filesystem nodes using runtime lambda generators, syncing VFS state to host storage for live telemetry and forensic inspection.

technical_projects.name: Real-Time Telemetry Engine
technical_projects.summary: A multi-source systems performance monitor with sub-second polling resolution and a pluggable output ecosystem supporting desktop widgets, status bars, and remote WebSocket streams.

features.text: Architected a DataStreamProvider model using std::unique_ptr and a PIMPL pipeline pattern to isolate data acquisition (CPU, memory, disk, network, process) from presentation logic.
Implemented jiffies-based delta polling for per-core CPU utilization and bytes-per-second network rates, with SSH-based remote collection via libssh.
Delivered structured output to multiple frontends simultaneously: Eww/Yuck desktop widgets, Waybar JSON tooltips, gtkmm widgets, and raw WebSocket/TCP streams.
Managed build and dependency graph via Nix Flakes for deterministic library versions across development and deployment environments.

technical_projects.name: Distributed Identity & Kerberos Pipeline
technical_projects.summary: An idempotent automation pipeline for synchronizing a distributed OpenLDAP directory and Kerberos KDC from a central manifest, supporting full RBAC and SSO infrastructure.

features.text: Engineered an LDAPTask framework with idempotent ensure_entry/delete_entry operations, applying modifications only when live state diverges from the manifest.
Implemented fine-grained ACLs securing Kerberos principal keys, and bootstrapped the KDC database within LDAP using kdb5_ldap_util.
Built an isolated slapd test harness that spawns and tears down ephemeral LDAP instances for CI/CD validation with full test purity.
Developed an LDAPVisualizer with an integrated fuzzer generating 500+ iterations of malformed DN strings to verify recursive tree-parsing stability.

technical_projects.name: Self-Hosted Infrastructure & Blog Engine
technical_projects.summary: A fully self-hosted personal infrastructure spanning a Markdown-driven blog engine, centralized SSO identity stack, and hardened multi-node server environment.

features.text: Engineered a Markdown-driven blog engine using Express.js and Handlebars, decoupling content from presentation and serving static-like delivery from a dynamic Node.js backend.
Implemented centralized identity management via OpenLDAP and Authelia with MFA-backed SSO, integrating SSSD and Kerberos across heterogeneous Debian and NixOS nodes.
Administered self-hosted Git, NGINX reverse proxy, and Nextcloud under a unified domain with Let's Encrypt wildcard TLS and mTLS enforcement on sensitive subdomains.
Managed client-side environment reproducibility via NixOS Flakes with remote access hardened through OpenVPN and SSH gateways.

technical_projects.name: Modular Pipeline Runner
technical_projects.summary: A task-orchestration engine that replaces shell-based build scripts with zero-subprocess overhead for Python-native coordination across cross-language build and test pipelines.

features.text: Designed a registry-based task hierarchy (SuiteTask/SuiteSubTask) with singleton initialization, dependency resolution, dry-run support, and staged execution lifecycle.
Implemented sh() and sh_thread() shell helpers with real-time I/O streaming, ANSI stripping, and structured ShellOutput for consistent cross-language error handling.
Validated task registry uniqueness, printer state, and bootstrap logic using property-based fuzzing via Hypothesis alongside standard pytest coverage.

technical_projects.name: Command Orchestration TUI
technical_projects.summary: A terminal-based command browser and execution environment with a modular plugin architecture for workspace discovery, filtering, and multi-mode subprocess launching.

features.text: Engineered an interactive TUI using prompt_toolkit with custom dialog logic, modal screens for command editing, and asyncio-driven non-blocking execution loops.
Architected a plugin-based workspace engine supporting Python, Nix, Node.js, and Shell strategies with a filtering pipeline of regex, gitignore, and extension-based rules.
Implemented a PTYManager for raw terminal forking and I/O streaming, with a CommandExecutor that auto-detects terminal emulators and handles PTY allocation for interactive processes.
Integrated a SafeHelpDiscovery layer providing multi-tier command introspection via man pages, Python AST inspection, and Firejail sandboxing.

technical_projects.name: Multi-Environment Proxy Redirection Layer
technical_projects.summary: A framework-agnostic HTTP proxy redirect system with a shared core logic layer and specialized adapters for both modern FastAPI and legacy CGI execution environments.

features.text: Architected a ProxyRedirectBase orchestrator encapsulating redirect state transitions and health-check logic independently of the execution environment.
Developed FastAPI and CGI subclass adapters that normalize divergent request/response models into a shared input contract without modifying core logic.
Engineered dynamic 301/302 redirect responses with HTML meta-refresh bodies, standardized CGI headers, and custom 503 error templates for unreachable backends.
Integrated a contextual debug logger targeting both a rotating log file and stderr fallback for tracing redirect flows across complex routing paths.

technical_projects.name: Infrastructure Health & Availability Pipeline
technical_projects.summary: A dependency-aware service health monitor with automated restart recovery, SMTP failure alerting, and a mock-heavy test suite validating multi-point failure scenarios.

features.text: Engineered a sequential dependency chain (slapd → SSSD → Authelia → NGINX) ensuring root-cause isolation before checking dependent application layers.
Implemented ServiceTask, PortTask, and ServicePortTask primitives combining systemctl status checks, TCP socket probes, and journalctl forensics on retry exhaustion.
Built a pluggable SMTP notification engine using Python dataclasses for clean separation of operational config from the core diagnostic logic.
Validated retry loops, socket failure simulation, and pipeline composition using unittest with heavy patch/Mock coverage across multi-point failure scenarios.

technical_projects.name: Full-Stack Accounting & Budgeting Engine
technical_projects.summary: A hierarchical budget and transaction management system built on recursive SQL logic and the double-entry accounting equation, currently being revived with a modernized backend and API-as-a-service architecture.

features.text: Architected a recursive CTE-based budget model resolving complex parent-child hierarchies with dynamic property inheritance across nested budget categories.
Enforced the double-entry accounting equation (Assets = Liabilities + Equity) at the data model level to guarantee structural financial integrity across all system nodes.
Built automated bank data ingestion via Puppeteer request interception, and a RESTful API with dedicated endpoints for full transaction lifecycle management.
Validated endpoint security and logic accuracy with a Mocha and Axios integration test suite covering GET/POST/DELETE cycles.

technical_projects.name: Lisp-Style Interpreter
technical_projects.summary: A lightweight, deterministic state engine and internal DSL built to explore language design, recursive descent parsing, and manual memory management in C.

features.text: Implemented a recursive descent parser producing a structured AST for a Lisp-style grammar.
Managed all memory allocation and deallocation manually, enforcing strict ownership and deallocation discipline.
Designed the interpreter as a deterministic state machine to explore minimal language runtime semantics.
